entities.raw_quote: The word onus appears at the edge of each wedge.

summary: This chunk contains several magical experiments from the Munich Handbook (CLM 849), including: a spirit conjuration circle with triple bands and Trinitarian inscriptions; a dream-based treasure-divination ritual invoking 'Oriens' and requiring penitential prayers, repeated invocations, and almsgiving; the great name 'Semiforas' used in magical operations; a horse-summoning rite involving Hebrew names written in bat blood and a binding incantation; protective spirit names for averting harm (Layalym et al.); an invisibility ritual using a white dove sacrificed on Saturday night and drawing with its blood; and a chemical recipe involving lunar salts, vitriol, mercury, wax, and various herbs/resins. Page references span 105v–108v.
key_passages: Hunc circulum facio in nomine Patris omnipotentis dei, qui solo verbo cuncta creauit...
per Sotuem, Sortfen... vt in nocte sequenti veniat ad me in sompnis Haram, spiritus benignus...
Coniuro te, eque bone, per creatorem celi et terre...
Nunc igitur sume albam columbam, die Sabbati de nocte, et cartam agni virgineam...
Item recipe salis petri, vitrioli, rorani,- ana tere, et fac aquam fortem ex eis...
